# Analysis — Secret History #1: How Power Works

- **video_id:** `lt8XLz78ZvY`
- **canonical_url:** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lt8XLz78ZvY
- **series / episode:** Predictive History — Secret History **#1**
- **raw_transcript / curated lecture:** [secret-history-01-how-power-works.md](../lectures/secret-history-01-how-power-works.md)
- **analyzed_at:** 2026-04-02 (operator / Volume III pilot)

## Thesis / aim

Opening **framework lecture**: Kant introduced as lens (noumena vs phenomena; time/space as forms of intuition; **reality as imagined / mediated**). Course structure = **past / present / future** with **geopolitical models**, **predictions** to test models, then **retrospective “secret history”** — thesis that **received history is false** or **implanted** by the powerful; **liberation** through understanding **how power works**. Extended **banking / money creation** thought experiment (deposits, loans, fractional reserve, receipts doubling gold, **bank runs**, **merchant–king** lending, **cartels / central banking**); **poverty and crisis** framed as **power-maintained scarcity illusion** vs infinite money creation.

## Defined terms (lecture usage)

- **Noumena / phenomena** — unknowable things-in-themselves vs world as it appears through perception.
- **Secret history** — corrective narrative uncovered after present/future analytic work (lecture arc).
- **Bank run** — simultaneous demand for redemption exposing over-issued claims.

## Claims (numbered) — see `claims/registry/claims.jsonl` clm-0035–0038

1. Kantian frame is used to privilege imagination and critical thinking over “what to think.”
2. Standard history/schooling is lecture-framed as implanted narrative serving power.
3. Banking section states deposit/loan arithmetic and Chinese megabank growth as pedagogical fact-claims in lecture voice.
4. Book layer should treat finance and macro claims as compression pending named sources.

## Internal tensions (if any)

- Kant exegesis is **highly compressed** — scholarly Kant reception is diverse; avoid attributing consensus to “Kant said X” alone.
- **“All history you know is false”** is rhetorically strong; mainstream historiography discusses bias, nation-building curricula, and archives without a single “implant” model.
- Banking mechanics (money totals, China example) need **dated** central-bank / monetary economics cites if used outside classroom voice.

## CIV-MEM lattice (light pass)

| Slot | Notes |
|------|-------|
| **Conditions** | High-school classroom; bilingual philosophical vocabulary; trust in Q&A. |
| **Institutions** | School curriculum, banks, central banking cartels, state–finance nexus (lecture frame). |
| **Seams / friction** | Student intuitions (“money scarce”) vs lecture’s “infinite money” hook — pedagogical tension by design. |
